assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The model explicitly states that TPU slices are recreated entirely in a single step (not rolling) and all pods are evicted simultaneously.

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The model warns that a maintenance event on any single slice in the 4-slice environment will trigger a cascading restart of all 4 slices.

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The model notes that all pods across all slices will be evicted and restarted at exactly the same time, killing the training job mid-flight.

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The model explains that if insufficient contiguous hardware is available, the slices will remain pending, causing hours or days of downtime.

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The model recommends manually triggering a model checkpoint and ensuring it is safely written to GCS before the maintenance.

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The model recommends manually scaling down the job to 0 and then manually scaling it back up to resume from the checkpoint, rather than configuring the framework for automatic checkpoint resume.

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The model suggests using a Maintenance Exclusion to block the upgrade and defer maintenance to finish the training run.

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The model mentions getting maintenance over with during a planned window and scheduling manual upgrades in the gaps between major training runs.
